instruction: What are the details of CVE-2022-4103?
output:
CVE-2022-4103 is a vulnerability in the Royal Elementor Addons WordPress plugin prior to version 1.3.56. This flaw results from the absence of authorization and Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) checks when creating templates. Consequently, any authenticated user, including subscribers, could exploit this vulnerability to create a post with an arbitrary title, potentially affecting site content integrity.

Classified under CWE-352, this vulnerability indicates insufficient verification by the web application of whether a request submitted was intentionally provided by the user. It poses a medium severity level risk and primarily impacts the Royal Elementor Addons before version 1.3.56.

To mitigate this vulnerability, it is recommended to apply updates to the latest version of the plugin. Leveraging advisory mitigation techniques such as M1048 and M1037 can help in minimizing potential exploitation risks. Additionally, monitoring web interactions for unusual activity and enhancing request validation processes are advisable.

This issue is associated with the MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1190, representing improper authorization management, possibly facilitating initial access or privilege escalation scenarios.
